title: Droogmansia stuhlmannii
taxonomy: Droogmansia stuhlmannii belongs to the family Leguminosae, also known as Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly referred to as legumes. Within the Leguminosae family, Droogmansia stuhlmannii is classified as a member of the subfamily Papilionoideae. The genus Droogmansia is part of the tribe Indigofereae. The specific classification of Droogmansia stuhlmannii within this tribe may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as being a part of the Indigofereae tribe. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics.

intro: Droogmansia stuhlmannii, also known as the African black bean, is a plant species belonging to the Leguminosae family, characterized by its distinctive black bean-like seed pods. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Droogmansia stuhlmannii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Droogmansia stuhlmannii is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used to make powdered extracts and capsules for its medicinal use.
